Title: Three cytochrome P450 enzymes consecutively catalyze the biosynthesis of furanoclerodane precursors in Salvia species

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidation module from kolavenol to salviarin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: abaxial leaf peltate trichomes; leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of annonene, an early salviarin precursor, from kolavenol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves; low in roots; co-expressed in SOM node 225
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves; low in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flowering-stage tissues; heterologous yeast galactose induction for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression discovery; yeast heterologous co-expression with SsKPS and SsKLS; GC-MS product identification against synthetic annonene; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for reconstructing early furanoclerodane pathway and biotechnological production of salvinorin-related scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Saspl_037721 encoded CYP76AH54, identified by SOM and mutual-rank co-expression with clerodane synthase genes. 2. In yeast, it converted kolavenol to annonene, confirmed by GC-MS and comparison with synthetic standard. 3. It defines the first P450 oxidation step toward salviarin precursors in S. splendens.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated initially as ferruginol synthase-like; evolved by neofunctionalization from ferruginol synthase-related CYP76AH genes.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidation module from annonene to salviarin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: abaxial leaf peltate trichomes; leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables accumulation of hardwickiic acid, a direct precursor in salviarin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves; co-expressed in SOM node 225
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flowering-stage tissues; heterologous yeast galactose induction for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MRA candidate selection; yeast co-expression assays; LC-MS and MS2 comparison with hardwickiic acid standard; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme for assembling salvinorin/salviarin precursor pathways in microbial or plant chassis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Saspl_010504 encoded CYP728D62 and was identified by co-expression with SsANS/SsKPS. 2. In yeast, it converted annonene to hardwickiic acid, verified by LC-MS and MS2 against authentic standard. 3. It defines the second consecutive P450 step in S. splendens furanoclerodane precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tandemly arrayed with SsHTAS; likely arose through duplication within CYP728D family.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidation module from hardwickiic acid to salviarin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes hardwickiic acid to hautriwaic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms downstream intermediate precursor toward salviarin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: abaxial leaf peltate trichomes; leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of hautriwaic acid, a salviarin precursor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves; SOM node 225
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flowering-stage tissues; heterologous yeast galactose induction for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast combinatorial co-expression with SsKPS, SsKLS, SsANS, and SsHDAS; LC-MS and MS2 comparison with authentic hautriwaic acid standard; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for full reconstruction of salviarin precursor pathway to hautriwaic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Saspl_010503 encoded CYP728D63, tandemly duplicated with SsHDAS. 2. It did not act on annonene alone but converted the SsHDAS product to hautriwaic acid in combinatorial yeast assays. 3. It defines the third consecutive P450 step toward salviarin in S. splendens.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts after SsHDAS; no hardwickiic acid formation when expressed without SsHDAS pathway context.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor toward salvinorin A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Psychoactive and therapeutic kappa-opioid receptor-active furanoclerodane; discussed for opioid addiction treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports annonene formation as an early intermediate in salvinorin A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peltate trichomes on abaxial leaf surface
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Peltate trichome transcriptome; heterologous yeast galactose induction for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology search in trichome transcriptome; yeast co-expression with SsKPS and SsKLS; GC-MS; coupled assay with SsHDAS yielding hardwickiic acid
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq trichome transcriptome, NCBI SRX1875790 / reads SRR3716680
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful ortholog for synthetic biology of salvinorin A precursors
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The S. divinorum ortholog of SsANS is Sd_DN33_c0_g1_i12/CYP76AH39. 2. In yeast, it produced annonene from kolavenol and, with SsHDAS, supported hardwickiic acid formation. 3. These results support annonene as a bona fide intermediate in salvinorin A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported as crotonolide G synthase (SdCS), but this study supports predominant annonene synthase activity under their assay conditions.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor toward salvinorin A
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Psychoactive and therapeutic kappa-opioid receptor-active furanoclerodane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports hardwickiic acid formation in the salvinorin A pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peltate trichomes on abaxial leaf surface
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Peltate trichome transcriptome; heterologous yeast galactose induction for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based candidate identification; yeast co-expression with SsKPS, SsKLS, and SdANS; LC-MS alignment with hardwickiic acid standard
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq trichome transcriptome, NCBI SRX1875790 / reads SRR3716680
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports reconstruction of early salvinorin A biosynthesis in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among three S. divinorum CYP728D candidates, only CYP728D26 showed HDAS activity. 2. Co-expression with upstream enzymes yielded hardwickiic acid in yeast. 3. This supports conserved early furanoclerodane biosynthesis across Calosphace species.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Other candidates CYP728D25 and CYP728D27 lacked HDAS activity in the reported assay.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for downstream P450s leading to salvinorin A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Psychoactive and therapeutic kappa-opioid receptor-active furanoclerodane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides kolavenol precursor for salvinorin A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-targeted enzyme; truncated transit-peptide-free protein used for assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays with recombinant proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology search; recombinant E. coli protein purification; coupled in vitro assay with SsKPS and GGPP; GC-MS detection of kolavenol
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq trichome transcriptome, NCBI SRX1875790 / reads SRR3716680
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables reconstitution of upstream salvinorin A precursor formation in synthetic systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdKSL1 was identified as a class I diterpene synthase closely related to S. splendens KLS enzymes. 2. Purified truncated SdKSL1 converted SsKPS-derived kolavenyl diphosphate to kolavenol in vitro. 3. This resolves previous uncertainty about KLS activity in S. divinorum.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Earlier studies failed to detect this activity; authors suggest different transit-peptide truncation choices likely explain the discrepancy.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for downstream P450s leading to salvinorin A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Psychoactive and therapeutic kappa-opioid receptor-active furanoclerodane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides kolavenol precursor for salvinorin A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-targeted enzyme; truncated transit-peptide-free protein used for assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays with recombinant proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology search; recombinant E. coli protein purification; coupled in vitro assay with SsKPS and GGPP; GC-MS detection of kolavenol
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq trichome transcriptome, NCBI SRX1875790 / reads SRR3716680
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream module for engineering salvinorin A precursor production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdKSL2, previously proposed but unconfirmed, was biochemically validated as a kolavenol synthase. 2. In vitro with SsKPS, it generated kolavenol from kolavenyl diphosphate. 3. Its high trichome expression supports a native role in salvinorin A precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most closely aligned to SspdiTPS1.5 among S. splendens class I clerodane synthases.
